note: Facets Pretty Paths adds one Facets url_processor plugin (id `facets_pretty_paths`) and one plugin type (the coder, `@FacetsPrettyPathsCoder` in Plugin/facets_pretty_paths/coder). It has no admin settings page: a facet source's `url_processor` property selects the processor, and each facet's coder is a third-party setting. The medium/hard cases are grounded in `facets_facet_source` and `facets_facet` config entities: the site ships no real facet source (no search display was built), so the setup/reset scripts create minimal standalone config entities to introspect / configure, then remove them. This exercises the same read/write config surface a real facet source uses.

evals.prompt: In the Drupal `facets_pretty_paths` module, what is the plugin id of the Facets URL processor it provides, and what is the id of the default Facets URL processor it replaces?
evals.expected_output: The module provides a Facets url_processor plugin with id `facets_pretty_paths` (annotation @FacetsUrlProcessor). It replaces the default `query_string` url processor from the Facets module.

evals.prompt: The `facets_pretty_paths` module defines its own plugin type used to turn a facet value into a URL segment. What is the plugin type called, what annotation class marks such a plugin, and which namespace/directory are these plugins discovered in?
evals.expected_output: The 'coder' plugin type: plugins are annotated with @FacetsPrettyPathsCoder and discovered in the Plugin/facets_pretty_paths/coder namespace, managed by plugin.manager.facets_pretty_paths.coder (CoderPluginManager), implementing CoderInterface (encode()/decode()). Bundled coders include default_coder, url_encoded_coder, taxonomy_term_coder, taxonomy_term_name_coder, node_title_coder and list_item_coder.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal site with Facets and Facets Pretty Paths installed, how do I make a given facet source use pretty (slash-separated) URLs instead of query strings? Name the config entity and the property that must change, and its target value.
evals.expected_output: Set the `url_processor` property of the facet source's `facets_facet_source` config entity (config name facets.facet_source.<id>) to `facets_pretty_paths` (default is `query_string`) and rebuild caches. In the UI: Admin > Config > Search > Facets > Facet sources > Edit > URL Processor > Pretty paths.

evals.prompt: This Drupal site has a Facets facet source with the id `eval_pretty_source`. Inspect the live configuration and tell me which Facets URL processor that facet source is configured to use.
evals.expected_output: The facets_facet_source config entity `eval_pretty_source` (facets.facet_source.eval_pretty_source) has url_processor = facets_pretty_paths, i.e. it uses the Pretty paths URL processor.

evals.prompt: On this site there is a Facets facet with the id `eval_coder_facet` on a pretty-paths facet source. Which Facets Pretty Paths coder plugin is configured for that facet? Inspect the live facet configuration to answer.
evals.expected_output: The facet `eval_coder_facet` stores its coder as the third-party setting facets_pretty_paths.coder = taxonomy_term_coder (facets.facet.eval_coder_facet.third_party.facets_pretty_paths.coder), i.e. the 'Taxonomy term name + id' coder.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site there is a Facets facet source config entity with id `eval_hard_source` that currently uses the default query-string URL processor. Configure it to use the Facets Pretty Paths URL processor so that facets on this source generate clean slash-separated paths, and apply the change to the live site.
evals.expected_output: The facets_facet_source `eval_hard_source` now has url_processor = facets_pretty_paths (changed from query_string) on the live site.
